topic: China SG null — verify post-c4 H10/H11 patches landed cleanly
change_summary: Decider self-applied 2 c4 patches: (a) H10 Level-3-residuals/raw-vs-residual conflation rewording (data/wins.json:WIN-014.detail_evidence ~line cited in commit 70ffbda); (b) H11 PRED-TIER-2 sign-convention harmonization to positive 10-13 µGal with explicit "per data/predictions.json" citation. Verdict change (ISS-1335, c4 H5) is held pending human review per queue 298 reason text. detail_evidence grew 4366→4609 chars (+5.6%) since c4.
our_argument_summary: The two patches landed but one of them introduces a new major grammar defect. H11 (sign convention) is a clean fix — WIN-014 now harmonizes to predictions.json positive convention with proper citation. H10 (Level-3 residuals wording) is conceptually correct (raw-vs-residual conflation removed, burden-shift framing added) but the sed-replace left the lead-in fragment "Superconducting gravimeters of this generation have" attached to the new clause, producing the broken sentence "Superconducting gravimeters of this generation have after tidal and atmospheric reduction, SG residuals reach the 0.1–1 µGal floor…" — an obvious user-visible grammar bug at the load-bearing point of the dome-vs-instrument argument. C4 holes H1 (SEC-8b.3 still enumerates E-PRED-A/B/C/D as live commitments — ISS-1825), H5 (verdict still "Misleading" while evidence argues self-contradiction — ISS-1335 pending-human), H3-partial (Sun 2010 attribution hedged but "no contact-phase anomaly was reported" still asserts affirmative absence — ISS-1828), H8 (eight-gravimeters provenance — ISS-1829), and H12 (TLDRs still reflect c2 framing — no tracked issue) all persist unchanged. The H11 patch also relocated rather than eliminated the cross-WIN sign inconsistency: WIN-014 is now correct, but WIN-012 still uses "−10 to −13 µGal" (5 occurrences in detail_evidence) without a verbatim-source citation, conflicting with predictions.json.

relevant_files: raw-text/monitor.py (no entry for WIN-014; tag "none" still correct)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-014 has no monitor.py entry. Static text claim only. The argumentative pipeline is now: (1) historical SG null (2009 China) cited as confirmation; (2) live PRED-TIER-3 commits to a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse in the same instrument class; (3) self-contradiction — both cannot be confirmations.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No comparison logic. The c5 question is whether our prose is grammatically intact, not whether dome code computes anything.
data_sources_used: purported Sun et al. 2010 (unverified; closest record cjg20130306, 2013)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: PRED-TIER-3 "Level-3 residuals" specification is the dome's natural escape route. The c4-recommended H10 rewrite addresses this by shifting burden to the dome to identify what processing step would hide a 10×–100×-noise-floor pulse — but the broken lead-in ("Superconducting gravimeters of this generation have after tidal…") muddles the rhetorical force of the burden-shift in the rendered prose.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None against monitor.py (still none). Cross-data-file: WIN-014 now correctly cites predictions.json positive convention; WIN-012 still uses negative sign without source citation. Cross-section: SEC-8b.3 still treats E-PRED-A/B/C/D as live (ISS-1825).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: raw-text/monitor.py contains no domain, no API hook, no Python check for WIN-014. Tag "none" confirmed across c2/c3/c4/c5 — invariant.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics expects null SG signal during eclipses (no recognized gravitational-shielding mechanism); the dome's claim that the null *confirms* its model is post-hoc reinterpretation of the default expectation. Borderline-but-defensible "false" — same call as c4. (Could argue "true" because the null IS the standard prediction and the dome is renaming "no signal" as "confirmation of dome dynamics," but that's a different tag-axis than the standard relabels_standard usage which targets renamed-mechanism cases like ionospheric→aetheric. Keep "false" for consistency with c4 framing.)
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Eclipse occurred 2009-07-22; dome model is much later. Whatever Sun et al. published, the dome adopted the result rather than predicting it in advance. Invariant across cycles.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: 0.0 µGal value is observation-adoption; no dome-geometry derivation exists. Invariant across cycles.

holes_found.description: The H10 patch left a dangling sentence-stub. Current detail_evidence reads literally: "…would have captured a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse if one existed. Superconducting gravimeters of this generation have after tidal and atmospheric reduction, SG residuals reach the 0.1–1 µGal floor (Van Camp, 2005; Crossley et al., 2013); a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse exceeding this floor by 10×–100× would be reportable even at Level-2 reduction; the burden is on the dome to identify what processing step would hide it." The fragment "Superconducting gravimeters of this generation have" is a subject+auxiliary verb with no completion — it was the leading clause of the c4 sentence ("…have raw noise floors below 1 µGal at minute timescales…") and the decider's sed-replace swapped the trailing content but did not strip the leading fragment. The reader sees broken English at the load-bearing point of the SG-vs-spring-gravimeter argument, immediately undermining the burden-shift the patch was supposed to deliver. Verified by direct diff inspection of commit 70ffbda (data/wins.json:WIN-014.detail_evidence). This is a regression introduced by the patch operation itself; c4 review is innocent.
holes_found.recommendation: Strip the dangling lead-in. Replace "Superconducting gravimeters of this generation have after tidal and atmospheric reduction, SG residuals reach the 0.1–1 µGal floor" with "After tidal and atmospheric reduction, SG residuals of this generation reach the 0.1–1 µGal floor" (or "Superconducting gravimeters of this generation, after tidal and atmospheric reduction, reach a residual floor of 0.1–1 µGal"). Either form preserves the c4 argumentative content while restoring grammar. This is a minor edit textually but a major findings-severity because the prose is plainly broken at a high-traffic location.

holes_found.description: C3/C4 H1 still open — tracked as ISS-1825 (open, major). SEC-8b.3 (data/sections.json:part8b.html) still enumerates the four E-PRED sub-tests as live dome commitments while WIN-014 detail_evidence asserts the live predictions page contains no E-PRED-C. One or the other must move. The queue 298 work did not address this — H1 is out-of-scope for the WIN-014 detail rewrite and lives in sections.json. Carrying forward unchanged from c4.
holes_found.recommendation: No action from the curmudgeon side beyond persisting the issue marker. ISS-1825 is open and decider-owned. Recommend the decider either (a) update SEC-8b.3 L3a to drop or re-frame E-PRED-A/B/C/D with a date-stamped note pointing to the 2026-03-12 raw-text snapshot as the authoritative enumeration, or (b) commission a fresh WebFetch of the dome predictions page to verify whether E-PRED-C is currently published.

holes_found.description: C3/C4 H5 (verdict mismatch) is held in ISS-1335 status "pending-human". Detail_evidence argues self-contradiction ("Whichever horn holds, the other fails"). Summary table verdict still reads "Misleading" (10 chars), finding still reads "Same logical contradiction as WIN-013" (37 chars), detail_verdict_text still reads "Same unfalsifiable structure as WIN-013…" (166 chars). WIN-012 is verdicted "Self-Contradicted" for an analogous structure. Persisting at the same severity (major) and not relisting in detail since the queue note is explicit that this is awaiting human disposition. C4 confidence on the verdict-change recommendation was 0.82; c5 confirms — no new evidence weakens or strengthens the call. Holding pattern is correct.
holes_found.recommendation: No additional action — wait for human disposition of ISS-1335. When the verdict change ships, expect a follow-up queue push to verify the c5-recommended companion edits (TLDRs, finding text, detail_verdict_text alignment) — they should ALL move when the verdict moves; piecemeal verdict change without TLDR/finding/text refresh would re-create the c4 H5 "surface fields lag detail" inconsistency.

holes_found.description: C3/C4 H3 is partially closed and remains tracked as ISS-1828 (open, moderate). The decider replaced "It saw none" with "No such identification has been offered; no contact-phase anomaly was reported." The first clause is a clean burden-shift; the second clause ("no contact-phase anomaly was reported") still asserts affirmative absence and inherits the same evidentiary gap c4 H3 flagged: if the Sun 2010 attribution is unverifiable, our claim that the campaign reported nothing rests on the same unverified document chain. The c4 recommended fix ("hedge the It-saw-none claim symmetrically") was applied to one of two affirmative claims. The hedge is half-complete.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch ISS-1828 by replacing "no contact-phase anomaly was reported" with "no published English-language contact-phase residual analysis from the 2009 campaign has been located" or equivalent symmetric hedge. This converts the WIN from asserting an empirical absence (which depends on the same unverified literature chain we critique) to asserting a publication absence (which is verifiable from our own search). The clause sits at the end of the H10-patched paragraph and does not interact with H13.

holes_found.description: The H11 patch closes the sign-convention inconsistency in WIN-014 itself by adopting predictions.json positive convention with explicit "per data/predictions.json" citation. But it does not propagate to WIN-012, which still uses HTML-entity-encoded "−10 to −13 µGal" / "−10 to −13 µGal" in 5 places of detail_evidence (verified by grep on data/wins.json:WIN-012.detail_evidence). The cross-WIN inconsistency is now: WIN-014 says positive 10–13, predictions.json says positive 10–13, WIN-012 says negative −10 to −13 — and WIN-014's ISS-1338 reconciliation note quotes the positive convention as the authoritative one. A reader who clicks from WIN-014 to WIN-012 encounters the conflicting sign without explanation. The c4 H11 recommendation said "two-step fix" — step 1 (verify dome's live convention) and step 2 (harmonize WIN-012 if predictions.json is authoritative). Step 1 was implicitly resolved (predictions.json adopted as authoritative); step 2 not executed.
holes_found.recommendation: Open a follow-up issue (or extend ISS-1338 if it covers cross-WIN sign harmonization) to update WIN-012 detail_evidence: replace each occurrence of "−10 to −13 µGal" or "−10 to −13 µGal" with "10–13 µGal" plus a parenthetical "(positive magnitude per data/predictions.json)" on first occurrence. Five replacements. This is a WIN-012 patch, not a WIN-014 patch — flagging here so the decider notices the propagation gap rather than treating the queue 298 closure as cross-WIN harmonized.

holes_found.description: C3/C4 H8 still open — tracked as ISS-1829 (open, minor). "Eight gravimeters were deployed across six sites in China for this eclipse" remains uncited. No action since c4. Carrying forward.
holes_found.recommendation: No new recommendation; ISS-1829 holds the original c3 fix.

holes_found.description: C4 H12 (TLDRs reflect c2 framing, do not surface PRED-TIER-3 self-contradiction) persists. tldr_evidence (239 chars) and tldr_verdict (99 chars) unchanged across c4→c5. No tracked issue exists for this — the c4 recommendation may have been folded into the H5 verdict-change package (TLDRs would refresh when verdict moves) but if H5 is held pending-human indefinitely, the TLDRs should not stay stale either. Surface here so the decider considers a TLDR-only patch decoupled from the verdict change if H5 disposition is delayed.
holes_found.recommendation: If ISS-1335 (verdict change) is going to land soon, fold TLDR refresh into that EXP. If ISS-1335 stays pending-human for >7 days, open a separate issue for TLDR refresh as a standalone minor patch. Recommended TLDR text: tldr_evidence — "The dome cites a null gravity result from a 2009 China superconducting gravimeter campaign as confirmation, but its current PRED-TIER-3 forward prediction calls for a 10–13 µGal pulse in the same instrument class at the August 2026 eclipse. The model cannot have a null and a positive prediction in the same instrument class as simultaneous confirmations." tldr_verdict — "Self-contradictory: 2009 null and Aug 2026 positive prediction in the same instrument class — whichever horn holds, the other fails."

stronger_arguments.description: The H10 patch added the strong sentence "the burden is on the dome to identify what processing step would hide it." When H13 is fixed (lead-in stripped), this sentence becomes the tightest single argument in the WIN. Consider promoting it to detail_verdict_text or to the TLDR — currently it is buried two-thirds of the way through detail_evidence and a casual reader will miss it. The strongest argument should not require a full read.

The PRED-TIER-3 self-contradiction can be sharpened by attaching a date-trigger: "If the August 2026 SG campaign returns a null below 10 µGal at C1/C4, the dome's historical-null-as-confirmation framing collapses; if it returns a 10–13 µGal pulse, the 2009 China SG campaign's reported null becomes the contradicting datum. Either outcome refutes one limb of the WIN-014 claim structure." This is the c4 advocate-mode preemptive recommendation phrased for inclusion. It converts the WIN from a static argument-from-prior-data into a clock-running self-execution test.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that historical superconducting-gravimeter observations during eclipses have generally returned null or near-null contact-phase signals (data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3.entry, raw-text/02-wins.txt eclipse-section). The 0.0 µGal claim is approximately correct as a description of state-of-the-art SG contact-phase records; this is invariant across cycles. The c4 H10 patch correctly substituted a defensible noise-floor characterization (Van Camp 2005; Crossley et al. 2013, both real-paper-verified per c4 citation_check) for the previously imprecise "raw noise floors" framing — that piece of the rewrite is solid.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The WIN structure cannot tolerate the dome's simultaneous commitments. WIN-011 (Mohe spring gravimeter, positive ~6.5 µGal) + WIN-014 (China SG 2009, null) + PRED-TIER-3 (positive 10–13 µGal in SG, August 2026) form an inconsistent triple unless the dome supplies advance criteria distinguishing eclipse-sensitive from insensitive instrument×site combinations — and no such criteria are published in raw-text/02-wins.txt or data/predictions.json. Being descriptively correct about the 2009 result does not rescue a claim structure that contradicts the dome's own forward prediction; the c4 detail_evidence makes the inconsistency explicit and the c5 H13 grammar fix would let it land cleanly. The H5 verdict ("Misleading" → "Self-Contradicted") would mark this on the surface for readers who do not click through — which is most of them.

deeper_analysis: C5 is a clean verification cycle on the queue 298 patches. Of the two patches the decider self-applied, one (H11 sign convention) is correctly executed at the WIN-014 level — the only follow-on issue is that the cross-WIN inconsistency moves from WIN-014↔predictions.json to WIN-012↔predictions.json, which is a WIN-012 problem now. The other (H10 Level-3 residuals wording) is conceptually correct but textually broken: the sed-replace left an orphaned sentence-stub. This is the single most important finding of this review, because it is a reader-visible grammar bug at a load-bearing location. The fix is mechanical (strip 9 leading words) and should ship as a minor_edit before any new work on this WIN. The structural state of the WIN — H1 sections.json desync, H5 verdict mismatch held pending-human, H3 affirmative-absence half-hedge, H8 uncited campaign description, H12 stale TLDRs — is unchanged from c4 and unaffected by these patches; those issues remain the decider's and the human operator's, not new curmudgeon work. The recommended_action for THIS cycle is "minor_edit" because H13 is the only thing the decider needs to act on immediately; everything else is either pending-human (ISS-1335), already tracked (ISS-1825/1828/1829), or surfaced for cross-WIN propagation (H14). Cycle-five state: WIN-014 detail_evidence is structurally strong, surface-level fields lag, and one regression introduced by the patch operation itself needs a one-line fix.

advocate_mode.best_defense: The strongest dome defense to the post-c4 WIN-014 is now narrower. The c4 H10 fix removes the easy raw-vs-residual exploit, and the H11 fix removes the cross-data-file sign-convention exploit. What remains is the H3-partial route: "You yourselves cannot verify the Sun 2010 attribution. Your sentence 'no contact-phase anomaly was reported' inherits that unverifiability — you are asserting absence of a finding that you also concede has no traceable English-language source. The proper claim is that no published analysis exists, not that no anomaly was found. Fix your epistemics before lecturing us on ours." This defense survives until ISS-1828 is closed cleanly; the half-hedge currently in place leaves it open. A secondary defense exploits the H13 grammar bug as a credibility hit: "The reviewer's prose is broken at the very point where they claim our model collapses — which side is doing careful work?"
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Three minor edits close both defense routes: (1) strip the H13 dangling fragment (kills the credibility-hit defense entirely; mechanical fix); (2) symmetrize the Sun 2010 hedge per H3-partial recommendation ("no published English-language contact-phase residual analysis from the 2009 campaign has been located" replaces "no contact-phase anomaly was reported"); (3) refresh TLDRs per H12 recommendation. The verdict change (H5/ISS-1335) and SEC-8b reconciliation (H1/ISS-1825) are independently important but not what a defender would attack first; the defender attacks the H3 epistemic asymmetry and the H13 prose breakage because those are the cheapest hits.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: C4 inconsistencies (1)–(3) re-evaluated post-patches: (1) WIN-012 verdict="Self-Contradicted" vs WIN-014 verdict="Misleading" for analogous structures — UNCHANGED, awaiting ISS-1335 disposition. (2) Sign-convention mismatch — RELOCATED, not eliminated: WIN-014 now matches predictions.json (both positive 10–13 µGal); WIN-012 still uses negative −10 to −13 µGal in 5 detail_evidence occurrences (HTML-entity-encoded as −10 to −13 µGal); the patch closed the WIN-014 limb of the cross-WIN inconsistency but opened the symmetric limb against WIN-012. (3) WIN-013 instrumental-hierarchy provenance shift — UNCHANGED, c4 noted that the 1,000× sensitivity comparison's basis shifted from "Van Camp 2001 four-SG vs Mohe" (c3 framing) to "this generation of SG vs LaCoste-Romberg" (c4 text); WIN-013 has not been updated. New (4): SEC-8b.3 (sections.json::part8b.html) still treats E-PRED-A/B/C/D as live dome commitments while WIN-014 says E-PRED-C is absent — tracked as ISS-1825.
cross_win_consistency.propagation_recommendation: Three follow-up patches recommended in priority order: (a) WIN-012 negative-sign harmonization (5 replacements; new ticket or extend ISS-1338); (b) SEC-8b.3 reconciliation (ISS-1825); (c) WIN-013 instrumental-hierarchy provenance refresh to match WIN-014's current framing.

claims_checked: H10 patch text: "after tidal and atmospheric reduction, SG residuals reach the 0.1–1 µGal floor" — landed correctly content-wise, broken grammatically by lead-in retention
H11 patch text: "PRED-TIER-2 (\"10–13 µGal gravity pulse at C1/C4\" per data/predictions.json)" — landed correctly
data/predictions.json::PRED-TIER-2.claim sign convention: positive 10–13 µGal — confirmed
data/predictions.json::PRED-TIER-3.claim sign convention: positive 10–13 µGal — confirmed
data/wins.json::WIN-012.detail_evidence sign convention: negative −10 to −13 µGal (HTML-entity-encoded) in 5 places — confirmed via grep
data/wins.json::WIN-014.detail_evidence character count: 4609 (was 4366 at c4) — +5.6% growth from H10/H11 patches
detail_verdict_text length unchanged at 166 chars — confirms H5 not yet executed
verdict field unchanged at "Misleading" — confirms H5 not yet executed (per ISS-1335 pending-human)
tldr_evidence (239 chars) and tldr_verdict (99 chars) unchanged across c4→c5 — confirms H12 persists
quantitative_verification.computations: No new numerical claims introduced by the c4 patches; the c4 quantitative verification of Van Camp 2005 / Crossley et al. 2013 noise-floor numbers (0.1–1 µGal residual at minute timescales) carries forward unchanged. The H10 patch correctly preserved the noise-floor citations.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Two: (1) H13 grammar bug — "Superconducting gravimeters of this generation have after tidal and atmospheric reduction, SG residuals reach…" — verified by direct read of data/wins.json:WIN-014.detail_evidence post-commit-70ffbda; (2) WIN-012 negative-sign convention persists despite WIN-014 / predictions.json adopting positive convention.

citations_verified: Van Camp 2005 — real paper, JGR Solid Earth, c4-verified (citation preserved in H10 patch)
Crossley, Hinderer, Riccardi 2013 — real paper, Reports on Progress in Physics 76:046101, c4-verified (citation preserved in H10 patch)
doi:10.6038/cjg20130306 — Chinese Journal of Geophysics 2013 paper, c4-verified
data/predictions.json::PRED-TIER-2 sign convention citation (added by H11) — exists, content matches
citations_failed: "Sun et al., 2010" — still unverified, hedge in place but covers only the attribution side, not the affirmative-no-anomaly side (H3-partial)
citations_unchecked: Specific page/figure references in 2013 CJG paper for C1/C4 contact-phase residuals (would require fetching the paper; out of curmudgeon scope)
